body_txt: When these people say they have a song in their heart, they really mean it. Characters with Musical Anatomy are those who have body parts that double as instruments. They may be robots, Mechanical Lifeforms , or otherwise constructed somehow, but they might also just be like that naturally. This can also extend to audio equipment like earphones and speakers. This is sort of an inherently whimsical trope and as such is most often seen in lighthearted or child-focused works. It's also usually reserved for minor characters, short works/sequences, or temporary transformations, probably because a character who is constantly making music could get grating fairly quickly. Noses that are also woodwind or brass instruments are particularly common, as are large stomachs making drum noises. Xylophones for Walking Bones has some overlap when the xylophone music is literally played on the skeletons' bones. Mickey Mousing and Disney Acid Sequence may ensue. Compare Everything Is an Instrument and iSophagus ; if you're dealing with a character who doesn't appear to have an instrument as part of their body but still sounds like one when they talk, that's Musical Instrument Voice . Musical Anatomy is frequently an attribute of Mooks in Band Land levels.
